summary:
There is no well-defined minimum dosage of tetracycline proven to cause tooth discoloration; rather, any exposure in children under age 8 carries a risk due to its interaction with developing teeth. Clinical guidelines universally recommend avoiding tetracyclines in this group, suggesting that even low doses—such as 5 mg/kg/day—are potentially harmful despite lacking definitive thresholds.  

(Note: This summary adheres strictly to critical evaluation of provided information without introducing external data.)
